headline: Staff Data Engineer | Streaming and batch pipelines, identity data systems, OLAP, and operational analytics
disciplineOtherNote: Staff-level data engineer focused on operational data systems, streaming/batch pipelines, and identity/security-adjacent platform data.
targetRolesNarrative: I am targeting staff data engineer and data platform roles building operational data systems, near real-time enforcement pipelines, feedback loops, and customer insights layers. I enjoy work where data is core to the product — streaming and batch paradigms, OLAP requirements, transactional identity services, and developer-facing data APIs.
industryOtherNote: Especially interested in identity, access, security, and agent-native platform companies.
companiesYouAdmire: Teams from Auth0, HashiCorp, Snyk, and Heroku-style platform companies that treat developer experience and operational data as first-class product concerns.
honestCareerNarrative: I began as a backend engineer building APIs and ETL jobs, then moved into data platform work when operational analytics became the bottleneck for product and security teams. At my current company I design streaming and batch pipelines that power near real-time enforcement, feedback loops, and customer insights for identity-adjacent services. I balance low-latency streaming with OLAP workloads and know when each paradigm fits.
proudestProfessionalWins:
Operational data platform: Designed pipeline and storage layers powering near real-time enforcement and customer insights for an identity platform used by multiple product teams.

Streaming + OLAP balance: Built systems serving transactional identity service needs and OLAP analytics for security posture reporting without compromising latency SLAs.

Developer experience: Delivered data APIs and observability that improved platform adoption and reduced support burden for internal teams.
gapsOrNonTraditionalPath: I transitioned from application backend to data platform after owning analytics bottlenecks for a security product line; that pivot became my long-term focus.
resumePlainText:
Jordan Kim
Staff Data Engineer — Identity & Operational Data Systems
Seattle, WA | US Remote

SUMMARY
Staff data engineer with 8+ years building streaming and batch pipelines, operational data systems, OLAP analytics, and data APIs for identity and security-adjacent platforms.

EXPERIENCE
Staff Data Engineer — Sentinel Identity — Remote | 2021 – Present
• Design and build operational data systems: pipelines, storage layers, and APIs for near real-time enforcement and customer insights.
• Build streaming and batch paradigms for identity services, knowing when low-latency streaming is essential versus batch analytics.
• Own OLAP requirements helping organizations understand and improve security posture.
• Partner with platform, security, and product teams on data models, feedback loops, and developer-facing APIs.
• Improve reliability, observability, and cost efficiency for high-volume event pipelines.

Data Platform Engineer — Mapline Data — Seattle, WA | 2018 – 2021
• Built Kafka-based ingestion pipelines and PostgreSQL/MongoDB storage layers for operational analytics.
• Migrated batch ETL workloads to containerized streaming workers on AWS.
• Introduced data quality checks and lineage documentation for critical pipelines.

PROJECTS
Guardrail Metrics Platform — Kafka, PostgreSQL, OLAP
Streaming and batch pipeline for near real-time enforcement metrics and customer insights dashboards.

Identity Data API — Go, PostgreSQL, Redis
Operational data APIs serving transactional identity services and analytics consumers.

EDUCATION
BS Computer Science — University of Washington — 2018

TECH STACK
Python, Go, SQL, Kafka, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, AWS, streaming/batch pipelines, OLAP, observability, data APIs
skillsCoreNarrative: Expert in streaming and batch data pipelines, operational data systems, OLAP analytics, PostgreSQL, Kafka, identity/security-adjacent data modeling, near real-time enforcement workflows, feedback loops, and developer-facing data APIs. Strong collaborator with platform, security, and product teams.
toolsOtherNote: Python, Go, SQL, Kafka, PostgreSQL, OLAP stores, streaming/batch orchestration, identity platform data modeling.
educationDetails: BS Computer Science, University of Washington (2018). Focus on databases, distributed systems, and data-intensive applications.

projects.title: Guardrail Metrics Platform
projects.summary: Streaming and batch pipeline platform for near real-time enforcement metrics, feedback loops, and customer insights dashboards. Balances low-latency streaming for operational decisions with OLAP workloads for security posture reporting.
projects.techStackExtra: Kafka, PostgreSQL, OLAP, AWS, observability, data APIs.
projects.impactMetrics: Powering operational analytics for identity and access workflows across multiple internal teams.

projects.title: Identity Data API
projects.summary: Operational data APIs serving transactional identity service needs and analytics consumers with clear SLAs, schema evolution practices, and monitoring.
projects.techStackExtra: Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, REST/gRPC, platform observability.
projects.impactMetrics: Reduced duplicate pipeline logic by centralizing data access patterns.

storyHardestTechnicalChallenge: A near real-time enforcement pipeline began missing SLA targets during traffic spikes due to hot partitions and expensive OLAP joins in the critical path. I split streaming and batch responsibilities, redesigned partitioning, and moved heavy analytics to asynchronous OLAP refresh jobs.
storyDisagreementOrConflict: Security wanted every event fully enriched synchronously before enforcement. I proposed a two-stage streaming design with minimal synchronous enrichment and async enrichment for analytics, preserving latency while meeting reporting needs.
storyBiggestMistake: I rolled out a schema change without sufficient backfill validation and caused temporary inconsistencies in customer insights dashboards. I added shadow pipelines and reconciliation checks afterward.
storyLeadingWithoutAuthority: Led a cross-team working group to define data contracts between identity services and analytics consumers, reducing breaking changes and support tickets.
storyTightDeadline: Delivered a critical enforcement metrics pipeline ahead of a major customer onboarding by scoping synchronous requirements first and deferring secondary dimensions.
storyConflictingPriorities: During a pipeline incident I deferred a platform feature demo and communicated a revised timeline after stabilizing lag and publishing remediation steps.
storyProcessImprovement: Introduced pipeline SLOs, lag alerts, and runbooks linked from dashboards, improving on-call effectiveness for data platform rotations.
storyDifficultFeedback: Was told my architecture docs assumed too much domain context. I added onboarding sections and glossary terms for identity and security concepts.
storyMentoringTeaching: Mentored engineers transitioning from backend to data platform work on Kafka tuning and OLAP vs streaming tradeoffs.
storyCrossFunctionalCollaboration: Partnered with platform, security, and product teams to define feedback loops and customer insights requirements for agent-native workflows.
storyAmbiguousProblem: Leadership asked why security posture metrics diverged across dashboards. I traced lineage issues to inconsistent enrichment timing and fixed pipeline synchronization.
storyEthicalOrRiskTradeoff: Declined to expose sensitive identity telemetry in a general analytics sandbox without proper access controls and audit logging.
whatYouWantNextNote: Want staff data/platform roles where operational data is core to the product, especially identity, access, and agent-native systems.
compensationExtraNote: Open to equity at early-stage platform companies; prioritize impactful data platform ownership.
workAuthOtherNote: US citizen authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
